name: task-list
description: Agent maintains a todo/task list within its own session â€” no subagents involved. The driver asks the agent to create three task items and walk each one pending â†’ in_progress â†’ completed. The point of the scenario: the items surface in the session API's `tasks` field with matching subjects and status, while SubagentCount stays 0 and state follows a normal ready â†’ working â†’ ready arc. Claude Code drives this via the Task tool (TaskCreate/TaskUpdate); OpenCode via todowrite (full-list replace). Distinct from foreground-subagent / background-subagent, which spawn real child sessions via the Agent tool.

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

Claude Code maintains a todo/task list within the agent's own session via the `Task*` tool family â€” `TaskCreate` adds an item (subject + description + activeForm), `TaskUpdate` moves an item between `pending` / `in_progress` / `completed`. These are internal to the session: no child session is spawned, no `ParentSessionID` link is created, and `SubagentCount` stays 0. That distinguishes this scenario from `foreground-subagent` / `background-subagent`, which use the Agent tool to spawn real child sessions â€” despite the shared 'Task tool' name.

What irrlicht observes: both the session state arc and the task-list contents. The claudecode parser emits a `TaskDelta` for each TaskCreate/TaskUpdate (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/claudecode/parser.go`), which the tailer folds into its running task list and reconciles against the `task_reminder` attachment snapshot (Claude Code's authoritative view of currently-tracked task IDs, issue #282). The result surfaces as `SessionMetrics.Tasks` (subject + status per item), and the replay golden now pins it via `summary.tasks`. The standard turn arc â€” user-line append drives ready â†’ working, terminal `stop_reason: end_turn` drives working â†’ ready â€” is load-bearing in the parser and unaffected by the task tools, which are auto-executed without a permission gate.
caveats: The tasks assertion lives in the replay byte-identity golden (summary.tasks), not the phase DSL â€” the validator's phase grammar has no task-form invariant, mirroring how basic-turn's TotalTokens assertion is verified structurally rather than by expected-validate.

The recording must end with a non-empty task_reminder snapshot for the items to surface â€” claudecode reconciles the running task list against the latest reminder, so a session that clears its list before the turn ends would leave summary.tasks empty.

sources.note: TaskCreate/TaskUpdate â†’ TaskDelta; handleAttachmentEvent reconciles against the task_reminder snapshot.
SessionMetrics.Tasks []Task â€” the API surface this scenario asserts on.

recipe.prompt: Use your task-list tool to create exactly three todo items, in this order: 'read README', 'summarize README', 'reply done'. Then work them one at a time: mark each item in_progress and then completed via the task-list tool before moving to the next. Do NOT use the Task/Agent tool to spawn any subagent â€” keep everything in this one session. When all three items are completed, reply with exactly the word: done
verify: transcript.jsonl contains three TaskCreate tool_use blocks (the three todo items) plus the TaskUpdate blocks that drive each to completed
transcript.jsonl contains NO tool_use block with name=Agent (no subagent spawned)
no parent_linked events appear and no subagent transcripts are written (SubagentCount stays 0)
the session API's tasks field lists the three items with matching subjects, all ending completed
state follows ready -> working -> ready; final session state is ready

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

OpenCode ships a first-class `todowrite` tool for reading and writing todo-list items. The tool is invoked synchronously within the parent session's turn â€” it does not spawn child sessions or DB rows. Each call appears in the transcript as `{type: "tool", tool: "todowrite", state: {status: "completed", input: {todos: [...]}}}` and carries an authoritative snapshot of the whole list (OpenCode rewrites the entire list on every call).

What irrlicht observes: both the session state arc and the todo-list contents. The parent session transitions ready â†’ working at the first `step-start`, stays working across the interleaved `step-finish reason=tool-calls` / `step-start` cycles (multi-step tool loop), and returns working â†’ ready at the final `step-finish reason=stop`. Separately, the opencode parser folds each `todowrite` snapshot into `SessionMetrics.Tasks` (subject + status per item, reconciled against the latest snapshot), so each item and its status (`pending` / `in_progress` / `completed`) surface in the session API's `tasks` field. The replay golden now asserts on that field â€” the committed recording ends with the three items at `completed` / `in_progress` / `pending` respectively, exercising all three statuses.

SubagentCount stays 0 and no child sessions appear â€” a task list is internal to the agent's own session, distinct from the Agent/subagent primitives covered by `foreground-subagent` / `background-subagent`.
caveats: todowrite calls appear in the transcript with status=completed immediately â€” there is no pending/running intermediate state visible to the parser because OpenCode resolves the tool synchronously before appending the part.

Because OpenCode rewrites the whole list per call, the parser reconciles each snapshot against the running task list (pruning vanished items, honouring status reversions) rather than applying incremental deltas.

sources.note: appendTodowriteDeltas converts each todowrite snapshot into TaskCreate/TaskUpdate deltas plus a TaskSnapshot for reconciliation.
SessionMetrics.Tasks []Task â€” the API surface this scenario asserts on.

preconditions: opencode CLI on PATH and authenticated (run a single message in opencode TUI to confirm)
irrlichd is recording (--attach or isolated) with the opencode SQLite watcher enabled
the default opencode model exposes the `todowrite` tool (most chat models do; lightweight 'codex' models may not)
setup: Driver runs `opencode` in tmux against <staging>/cwd so each scenario gets a fresh project directory. OpenCode appends to the global ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db; sessions are isolated by their session id, not by cwd.

script.text: Use your todo list (the todowrite tool) to track three short work items, updating each item's status (pending â†’ in_progress â†’ completed) as you make progress. Do NOT spawn any subagent. Then reply with exactly the word: done

verify: events.jsonl shows the session entering ready, then a single ready->working transition
the session's transcript (SQLite parts) contains at least one tool part with tool=todowrite carrying the three-item snapshot
no child session rows appear in ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db with parent_id = this session id (SubagentCount stays 0)

the session API's tasks field lists the three tracked items with their statuses surfacing (the committed recording ends with one completed, one in_progress, one pending â€” exercising all three statuses)
events.jsonl shows a working->ready transition at turn end; final session state is ready

metadata.notes:
agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a
applicable: no

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a
applicable: no

## Reasoning

The `task-list` scenario exercises the **task-list-management** primitive: the agent maintains a todo/task list inside its own session (no child sessions), walking items `pending -> in_progress -> completed`, and those items surface in the session API's `tasks` field while `SubagentCount` stays 0 and state follows a normal `ready -> working -> ready` arc. The canonical reference implementations are Claude Code's `TaskCreate`/`TaskUpdate` tools and OpenCode's `todowrite` (full-list replace).

### agent_supports: no

Pi ships **no built-in task-list / todo tool**, and this is a deliberate design decision by the author, not an oversight:

- The Step 2.5 keyword guard against the installed binary (`@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` v0.70.0, `dist/` bundle) found zero hits for `todoWrite`/`taskCreate`/`taskUpdate`/`task_list`/`tasklist`/`/tasks`/`/todo`. The only `todo` matches were `TODO:`/`FIXME:` patterns in a vendored syntax highlighter, and `taskkill` (the Windows process-kill command used inside `bash`) â€” neither is a task-list tool.
- The built-in tool registry (`dist/core/tools/`) is exactly: `bash`, `edit`, `find`, `grep`, `ls`, `read`, `write`. `pi --help` summarises this as "AI coding assistant with read, bash, edit, write tools." The slash-command set (`/compact`, `/model`, `/new`, `/resume`, `/fork`, `/export`, `/session`, ...) contains no `/tasks`, `/todo`, `/plan`, or `/checklist`.
- Pi's author states explicitly that pi "does not and will not support built-in to-dos" because "to-do lists generally confuse models more than they help." The documented workaround is a `TODO.md` file or a third-party extension (e.g. `pi-manage-todo-list`, which registers a `manage_todo_list` tool).

A third-party extension *could* add a todo tool, but the scenario asks about the agent's own behaviour out of the box, and out of the box pi has no such capability â€” hence `agent_supports: no`, which freezes the pipeline regardless of `irrlicht_observes`.

### daemon_capability: n/a; driver_capability: n/a

Even setting the agent question aside, irrlicht's pi parser cannot populate the `tasks` field. The daemon's `tasks` field (`core/cmd/replay/types.go:129-135`) is fed only by tailer `TaskCreate`/`TaskUpdate` deltas (and a `TaskSnapshot`), which today are produced exclusively by the claudecode parser (`TaskCreate`/`TaskUpdate` cases, parser.go:390/400) and synthesised by the opencode parser from `todowrite` snapshots (parser.go:244, metrics.go). The pi parser (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go`) only extracts generic `ToolUses{ID,Name}` from `toolCall` content blocks and emits `assistant`/`turn_done`/`user_message`/`tool_result` events â€” it has **no** task-list code path and emits no `TaskCreate`/`TaskUpdate`/`TaskSnapshot`. So even if pi (via an extension) emitted a `manage_todo_list` tool call, irrlicht would record it as an opaque tool use and the session `tasks` field would stay empty. The other user-observable signals (`SubagentCount` 0, `ready -> working -> ready`) would hold trivially, but the load-bearing assertion â€” items in `tasks` with subject + status â€” would not.

## Capability-key mapping (Step 4)

The Primitive exercised is "task-list management internal to the agent's own session." There is **no matching key** for this primitive in `replaydata/agents/features.json` (the catalog's capability namespace is `headless_mode, tui, tool_calls, permission_hooks, subagents, session_id_assignment, settings_file_support, session_resume, mcp_servers, streaming_output, structured_output_jsonl, slash_commands, plan_mode, git_integration, multi_model_orchestration, repo_map, voice_input, multimodal_input, gui_mode, watch_mode, linting_test_automation, edit_strategy_selection, context_curation, cost_tracking`). `subagents` is explicitly the wrong key (the scenario is defined as NOT spawning child sessions), and `plan_mode` is a read-only/advisory mode, not list tracking. The verdict above is therefore grounded in the binary + parser evidence, not a capability flag.

## Structural concerns (flagged to maintainer)

1. **Dangling `requires` capability id.** `scenarios.json` declares `task-list` with `requires: [task_list]`, but no `task_list` key exists in `replaydata/agents/features.json`. Matrix applicability for this row is **definitionally undefined** until the maintainer adds a `task_list` capability key (with a per-agent flag) to `features.json`.
2. **Catalog divergence.** `task-list` is present in `scenarios.json` but **absent from the 38-id `agent-scenarios-coverage.json` matrix** â€” `slice-cell.sh` returned "no coverage entry for this (scenario, adapter)". The maintainer should reconcile the two catalogs (add the row to coverage, or remove it from scenarios.json if intentionally dropped).
caveats: Scenario `requires: [task_list]` references a capability id `task_list` that does NOT exist in replaydata/agents/features.json â€” matrix applicability is definitionally undefined until the maintainer adds the key.
task-list is in scenarios.json but ABSENT from agent-scenarios-coverage.json (the 38-id matrix) â€” a catalog divergence the maintainer should reconcile before any column scan can include this cell.

Pi is extensible: a third-party extension (e.g. pi-manage-todo-list, registering a manage_todo_list tool) can add todo tracking. This assessment judges stock pi (v0.70.0); the verdict does not cover extension-augmented installs.
Even with an extension emitting task calls, irrlicht's pi parser has no TaskCreate/TaskUpdate/TaskSnapshot path, so the session `tasks` field would stay empty regardless.

sources.note: Pi author: pi 'does not and will not support built-in to-dos' â€” 'to-do lists generally confuse models more than they help'; recommends a TODO.md or an extension.
npm package overview; built-in tools are read/write/edit/bash, capabilities added via skills/extensions/packages â€” no built-in todo.
Third-party Pi extension that ADDS todo-list management (manage_todo_list) â€” confirms it is not built in.

Stock pi v0.70.0 built-in tool registry: bash, edit, find, grep, ls, read, write â€” no task/todo tool. `pi --help` confirms 'read, bash, edit, write tools' and the slash-command set has no /tasks /todo /plan.

Pi parser extracts only generic ToolUses{ID,Name}; emits assistant/turn_done/user_message/tool_result â€” no TaskCreate/TaskUpdate/TaskSnapshot, so the daemon `tasks` field cannot be populated for pi.
Lines 129-135: the session `tasks` field is fed from tailer TaskCreate/TaskUpdate deltas (claudecode direct, opencode synthesised from todowrite) â€” neither path exists for pi.
Capability namespace has no `task_list` key â€” the scenario's requires:[task_list] is a dangling reference.
38-scenario matrix; task-list is absent (present in scenarios.json) â€” catalog divergence.
